title: Matar Paneer (Peas and Cottage Cheese Curry)

ingredients.title: For the Paneer and Peas
items: 250 g paneer (cottage cheese), cut into 1-inch cubes
1–2 tsp oil or ghee for sautéing

ingredients.title: Spices
items: 1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
½ tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves)

ingredients.title: Other
items: ½ cup fresh cream or 2 tbsp yogurt (optional, for richness)
Fresh coriander leaves, chopped for garnish

instruction.title: Instructions
steps: Prepare the Paneer and Peas: Heat 1–2 tsp oil in a pan on medium flame.
Lightly sauté the paneer cubes until golden brown on all sides (optional). Set aside.
Blanch peas in boiling water for 2–3 minutes if using fresh; frozen peas can go straight into the curry.
Make the Masala Base: In the same pan, heat 2 tsp oil. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Stir in ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1–2 minutes until raw aroma disappears.
Add tomato puree, turmeric, red chili, coriander, and cumin powders. Cook until oil separates from the masala.
Cook the Curry: Add 1 cup water to make a smooth gravy.
Stir in peas and paneer cubes. Simmer for 5–7 minutes on low-medium heat.
Add garam masala and kasuri methi. Mix gently.
For richer taste, stir in cream or yogurt. Adjust consistency with water.
Garnish and Serve: Garnish with chopped coriander leaves.
Serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

chefTips: Use fresh, soft paneer for a creamy texture.
Kasuri methi adds authentic flavor; crush it between your palms before adding.
For extra depth, roast spices lightly before adding to the masala.
